response:
I've drafted and saved the decision as an ADR. Note: `node pdca-workflow/scripts/adr-lint.mjs docs/decisions` keeps requiring approval in this session and I haven't been able to run it — please approve or run it yourself to confirm the new ADR passes lint before merging.

## Decision: defer adoption, authorize the pre-registered test

Recorded as `docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-do-phase-defer-pending-test.md`:

**Do not adopt tiered-agent execution now.** No empirical data exists — I searched `benchmarks/` and found no tiered-execution run has ever been done. ADR 0024 already commits this repo to "do not adopt a cost-side artifact on assertion," and a tiered execution architecture (Sonnet plans/validates, Haiku implements) is exactly that kind of claim ("a validator catches the cheap worker's drift") in a different shape — it inherits the same empirical bar.

**What I did authorize instead**, resolving the three "owner calls before spend" that issue #41 Comment 3 left open:
1. **Grader independence** — accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual (same precedent ADR 0019 already accepts); not worth a 4th tier just for grading.
2. **Hermetic controls** — carry over CLAUDE.md relocation; drop tool-deny as unenforceable for tool-using tasks (documented as a known threat, not a blocker).
3. **Retry/feedback** — keep the pre-registered ≤3-cycle cap; validator feedback returns only the diagnosed gap, never a rewritten solution (mirrors ADR 0024's diagnose-before-edit method).

**Adoption itself stays gated** on the pre-registered decision rule from Comment 1: adopt Tiered only if pass-rate delta ≥ -0.05 vs Sonnet-solo AND tokens ≤0.7× (or wall-clock ≤0.7×). The test runs after the current skills/agents benefit-benchmark batch completes (per the issue's own sequencing), and the result gets recorded either way per ADR 0024's append-only rule — this ADR gets rationalized in place with the outcome rather than superseded by a new one.

Next step for you: `git add docs/decisions/0035-*.md ISSUE-41.md` (or discard the untracked issue file if it was scratch) and open this as its own PR per the one-ADR-per-PR rule, once lint is confirmed clean.

artifacts.docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-do-phas:
---
id: 0035
title: "Tiered-agent Do-phase execution: defer adoption pending the pre-registered empirical test"
status: accepted
summary: "Do not adopt tiered-agent (Sonnet-plans/Haiku-implements) execution for pdca-workflow's Do phase on assertion (ADR 0024); authorize running issue #41's pre-registered 4-arm test, with its three pre-spend owner calls resolved here, and gate actual adoption on the test's decision rule."
---

# 0035 — tiered-agent Do-phase execution: defer, authorize the test

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: owner-directed — issue #41's comments 1-3 already pre-registered the test design; the
  controlling bar is the existing ADR 0024, so no separate advisor panel was needed.
- Context: issue #41 proposes redesigning the Do phase (tech-lead -> implementation) so a top-tier
  model plans/validates and a cheap model implements the bulk of the work. Comment 1 pre-registers
  a falsifiable 4-arm test (Haiku-solo / Sonnet-solo / Opus-solo / Tiered) against the existing
  hermetic harness (`benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic/`). ADR 0024 forbids adopting a
  cost-side change on assertion. No `benchmarks/*tiered*` directory exists (checked 2026-07-14) —
  the test has not run. Comment 3 leaves 3 owner calls open before spend.

## Decision
1. Do **not** adopt tiered-agent execution now — no data exists yet (ADR 0024: "do not adopt on
   assertion" applies equally to a cost-side execution-architecture claim).
2. Authorize running the Comment 1 pre-registered test as the next step on this issue, sequenced
   after the skills/agents benefit-benchmark batch per issue #41's own "Timing" note — verify that
   batch's completion at test-start time, not here.
3. Resolve the 3 pre-spend owner calls (Comment 3) so the test can start without further gating:
   a. **Grader independence** — accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual (the Tiered arm's Sonnet
      main is graded by the Sonnet blind grader); same residual ADR 0019 already accepts elsewhere.
      Do not spin up a 4th tier solely to grade.
   b. **Hermetic controls** — carry over user-CLAUDE.md relocation; drop tool-deny (unenforceable
      once the task is tool-using implementation) — record as a known threat in the run write-up,
      not a blocker.
   c. **Retry-cap** — <=3 cycles (already pre-registered, Comment 1). **Feedback format** — the
      validator returns only the diagnosed gap (which expectation failed + why), never a rewritten
      solution — mirrors ADR 0024's diagnose-before-edit method and keeps the worker's context
      from growing across cycles.
4. Actual adoption stays gated on the Comment 1 decision rule: Tiered adopted only if pass-rate
   delta >= -0.05 vs Sonnet-solo AND tokens <=0.7x (or wall-clock <=0.7x). Record the result either
   way (append-only, ADR 0024), including whether Tiered reaches Opus-solo quality at sub-Opus cost.

## Justification
ADR 0024 is the controlling precedent: this repo already committed to measuring benefit-per-token
before trusting a cost-side claim for any context-cost artifact. Tiered execution is the same
claim ("a top-tier validator catches a cheap worker's drift") in a different shape, so it inherits
the same bar. Approving the test now — rather than deferring the whole issue — keeps momentum
while withholding the adoption call until the pre-registered criterion resolves. Cost is near zero:
the test reuses the hermetic harness, `blind.py`/`aggregate.py`, and `grade.workflow.js` verbatim
(Comment 3); the risk this forecloses is silently paying the tiering orchestration tax without a
quality-matched saving to show for it.

## Rejected alternatives
- Adopt now on the design's face plausibility — violates ADR 0024 directly; "a validator will
  catch it" is exactly the kind of claim ADR 0024's hermetic measurement exists to check, not assume.
- Reject/close the issue outright — premature; the design has not been tested, only asserted;
  closing without data discards a real cost lever with no evidence either way.
- Block the test until a genuinely independent 4th grading tier exists — over-engineers grader
  independence for a benchmark scoped as directional (3 reps, 8 tasks); ADR 0019 already accepts
  this residual elsewhere, so a new bar here would be inconsistent.

## Assumptions
- [checkable] no `benchmarks/*tiered*` directory or ADR referencing issue #41 exists yet — verified
  via repo search 2026-07-14 (`ls benchmarks/`, `docs/decisions/` up to 0034); result: confirmed absent.
- [checkable-doc] the skills/agents benefit-benchmark batch issue #41 sequences after has a
  completion signal (merged PR/ADR) before the tiered test starts — owner verifies at test-start
  time, not here.
- [unverifiable] the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual (3a) does not bias the Tiered arm's measured
  pass rate upward relative to the other solo arms — REOPEN-IF: a later run with a genuinely
  independent 4th-tier grader shows a directional gap vs this run's numbers.

## Revisit triggers
- The pre-registered test completes -> rationalize this ADR in place with the adopt/reject result
  and the measured numbers (ADR 0024's record-either-way rule); do not open a second ADR for the result.
- Someone tries to start the tiered test before the skills/agents benefit-benchmark batch this
  issue is sequenced after has completed -> block start, finish that batch first.
